display_name: Always check system uptime before interpreting any cumulative counter
context: Any troubleshooting session where cumulative counters are being evaluated
observation: Counter values are presented without context on how long they have been accumulating
naive_interpretation: The counter value represents the current state or recent behavior of the system
correct_interpretation: Cumulative counters span the entire period since boot or last clear. A device with 180 days of uptime has counters covering 6 months of operation. The first step before interpreting any counter is to establish the accumulation window by checking uptime and last counter clear time.
why: Without knowing the accumulation window, counter values are uninterpretable — 100 drops in 1 minute vs 100 drops in 6 months are completely different situations
Some counters reset on reload but not on failover, creating inconsistent windows across a redundant pair
The clear counters command may have been run at an unknown time by another operator, making the window even less predictable

confirmation_commands.purpose: Establish the maximum possible counter accumulation window
Establish the maximum possible counter accumulation window
Establish the maximum possible counter accumulation window

confirmation_commands.command: show interface Ethernet1/1 | include Last clearing
confirmation_commands.purpose: Check when counters were last cleared — if never, they span full uptime

traps: Interpreting counters without first establishing the time window
Assuming counters reset after a software upgrade when only a reload clears them
Not realizing that ISSU (in-service software upgrade) may not reset counters on some platforms
Comparing counters between two devices with different uptimes as if they are directly comparable
